title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
summary: Double iframe architecture isolates MCP and ChatGPT apps by nesting an srcdoc iframe inside a controlled-subdomain iframe. This pattern prevents Content Security Policy bypasses, credential theft, and sandbox escapes by separating app code (inner frame) from script loading (outer frame). Each app gets a unique subdomain to prevent cross-app storage collisions.
writeup: Frédéric Barthelet of Alpic explains the double iframe architecture used to embed MCP and ChatGPT apps, which nests an srcdoc iframe inside an iframe served from a controlled subdomain. The split assigns distinct roles: the inner frame holds app code while the outer frame handles script loading, separating concerns that would otherwise enable Content Security Policy bypasses, credential theft, and sandbox escapes. The talk also covers why each app receives a unique subdomain, which prevents cross-app storage collisions. The takeaway is a concrete isolation pattern for app marketplace architecture grounded in CSP and sandboxed iframes.
